rationale: The citing paper does not use Petrin and Train (2010) in its empirical design or estimation; it implements an instrumental-variables approach for endogeneity instead. Petrin and Train is cited only once in the limitations/future-research discussion as an example of a possible alternative endogeneity correction method. Because the citation serves as a generic methodological pointer rather than informing the paper's actual framework or results, its role is a passing mention.
